title: Terrorist Finance Tracking Program (TFTP)
summary: This source describes the Treasury's Terrorist Finance Tracking Program (TFTP), a counter-terrorism initiative that uses SWIFT financial messaging data to trace terrorist financing networks. The content is about national security policy and has no relevance to market structure or macro catalysts for financial market research.
key_points: TFTP was initiated after September 11, 2001 to identify and track terrorists including Al-Qaida, Hizballah, and HAMAS networks
Treasury issues subpoenas to SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) for financial transaction data on suspected terrorists
Since 2010, SWIFT data has been stored in the European Union under a U.S.-EU agreement
The program operates under legal authority including IEEPA and UNPA, plus Executive Orders
SWIFT is overseen by a committee including the Federal Reserve, Bank of England, ECB, and Bank of Japan
The program includes privacy safeguards with independent audits and systematic logging of searches
why_it_matters: This source is not relevant to financial market research. It describes a national security program and does not contain information about market structure, monetary policy, economic data releases, or other macro catalysts that affect financial markets.
